upcoming.subject: Inside SBM - A Day at a Campus

cmt.wins:
- One episode delivered and ready for upload to Masters Of The Market this week.
- Ishika leading; clear ownership on pod.
cmt.risks:
- Zero on-time delivery rate signals systematic delays in this pod's workflow.
- Episode sat in 'Awaiting Upload' status; upload date already passed (12 Apr).
- No shoot-to-live visibility; can't diagnose where 8-week pipeline is breaking.
cmt.action:
- Ask Ishika: why is the CXO episode not live yet, and what's blocking upload today?
- Pull shoot and edit timings for this episode to find the bottleneck.
- Confirm upload slot booked for Masters Of The Market this week or flag as at-risk.

perf.wins:
- Conversion rate at 0.83 is stellar; Performance Ads is moving product.
- 20 delivered in last 12 weeks shows consistent output under Devansh's lead.
perf.risks:
- On-time delivery at 33% is a crisis; 2 in 3 shoots miss deadline.
- 5 awaiting edit and 1 in editing signals editing bottleneck, not shooting.
- 12.6 days edit-to-delivery is the slow step; shoot-to-edit only 4.8 days.
- No planned delivery dates logged; cannot forecast next week's releases.
perf.action:
- Ask Devansh: who owns editing and why is the handoff breaking?
- Pull the 5 awaiting edit projects; identify the blocker by Wednesday.
- Rebuild delivery forecasting this week; Performance Ads cannot ship blind.
- Protect May-June momentum; losing on-time rate undermines client trust.

bharat.wins:
- Both Travel & Learn episodes live; 100% conversion rate shows Sabhya executes.
- Edit-to-delivery turnaround is sharp at 10 days; post-production efficiency is real.
bharat.risks:
- On-time rate at 0% is a red flag. PT5 missed by 19 days, PT6 by 13 days.
- Only 2 active projects across 12 weeks suggests pipeline drought; Bharat.mu is understaffed or under-briefed.
- Shoot-to-edit at 25.5 days is the bottleneck; raw footage sits too long.
bharat.action:
- Ask Sabhya: what's delaying edits from shoot? Is it review cycles or resource constraint?
- Map next 8 weeks now. Two projects in 12 weeks means Bharat.mu isn't scaling the way the business needs.
- Check if Builders.mu or Brand/Ad Films are poaching editors or shooting schedules from Bharat.mu.

seriesc.wins:
- Ishika's YT - Podcasts (Series C) hit 100% on-time rate: 12 active, all scheduled.
- Shot-to-live averaging 68 days shows consistent pipeline discipline.
- One piece already delivered, waiting upload—momentum intact.
seriesc.risks:
- Zero conversions (0.0 rate) signals content isn't landing or upload workflow broken.
- 11 of 12 pieces stuck in edit limbo—where's the bottleneck?
- Episode names still blank across recent batch; metadata chaos incoming.
- Upload dates span March–April 2026 but no planned delivery dates logged anywhere.
seriesc.action:
- Ask Ishika: why is conversion rate 0%? Is it upload timing, content fit, or tracking?
- Pull edit queue status now—what's blocking 11 episodes from moving to delivery?
- Assign episode names this week before upload phase; prevent upload team surprises.
- Map shoot-to-edit duration (currently null); find the real constraint.

builders.wins:
- Builders.mu shipped 17 live projects; conversion rate of 0.81 shows strong client satisfaction.
- Edit-to-delivery cycle is tight at 3.6 days; Raja and Ratnam executing consistently.
builders.risks:
- On-time rate of 59% is the real problem; half your projects miss planned delivery by days.
- Four projects stuck in 'Shot, Awaiting Edit' limbo; 9-day shoot-to-edit lag is your bottleneck.
- June pipeline has dried up (zero ships 4-11 Jun); capacity planning broke somewhere in May.
builders.action:
- Divyam: ask Raja why edit queue swells to 9 days when delivery happens in 3.6 days after.
- Stop shipping late as normal; audit the four pending projects and unblock edit capacity this week.
- Rebuild June forecast now; you have 21 active but visibility beyond mid-May is gone.

offcampus.wins: - YT - Off Campus has one asset shot and in edit (Muradabad Brass). - Series shows recent activity after dormancy; momentum building.
offcampus.risks: - Only 1 active asset across 12 weeks suggests chronic underproduction. - No delivery date set; 'Shot, Awaiting Edit' limbo since December shoots. - Zero shipped this cycle; no conversion or velocity metrics to track.
offcampus.action: - Ishika: what's blocking Muradabad edit? Get a hard delivery date today. - Why only 1 active? Reload the pipeline with 2-3 shoots lined up for April. - Set weekly YT - Off Campus standup; you need visibility on edit bottleneck.

overall.note: Studio is eerily quiet: zero projects across all pods this week, yet 14 shoots lined up. Something's broken in tracking or intake. Paradox Weekly hemorrhaging subscribers fast. Nandini's momentum is real but fragile. Divyam: demand a 48-hour pipeline audit and subscriber churn root cause.
overall.wins:
- Nandini's NL hits 34.9% open rate: best-in-class storytelling driving engagement.
- Influencer portfolio scaled to 82 campaigns across six agencies without margin squeeze.
- ORM shows 19% positive sentiment across 50M+ views; Reddit engagement stable at 72K.
overall.risks:
- All four creative pods at zero active projects this week. Pipeline visibility collapsed.
- Paradox Weekly subscriber base fell 27% in four weeks (410K to 296K). Retention crisis.
- Swati's NL and Nandini's NL both show sharp drops post-May 27. Campaign fatigue or technical issue?
- Builders.mu, Brand/Ad Films, Performance Ads: no on-time delivery data. Accountability gaps.
overall.today:
- Ask Raja Kumar and Devansh Kotak: what ships next week? Zero active doesn't mean zero work.
- Ping Pratham Mittal on Paradox Weekly bleed. Is it content, sendability, or segment erosion?
- Check Ishika's YouTube pods. Eight shows, zero shipped this cycle. Backlog or cancellations?
